name: Travis
description_short: Travis Credit Union Park was a defunct minor league baseball stadium in Vacaville, California that operated from 2000-2008. This is not a financial services company.
description_long:
Travis Credit Union Park was a 2,800-capacity baseball stadium located at 50 Nut Tree Road in Vacaville, California. The facility opened on May 26, 2000, following ground-breaking in November 1999, with a construction cost of $4.5 million. It was primarily used as a minor league baseball venue and served as the home field for two teams: the Solano Steelheads of the Western Baseball League (2000-2002) and the Solano Thunderbirds of the Sierra Baseball League (2003-2007).

The stadium offered standard baseball venue amenities including natural grass playing surface, concessions facilities, and a picnic area. The facility underwent renovations in 2001 when bleachers were converted to bucket seats, and again in 2002 when a new concessions facility and picnic area were added. The ballpark featured field dimensions of 330 feet down the left field line, 405 feet to center field, and 330 feet down the right field line.

The stadium's operational life was limited. In 2008, a deal was reached to dismantle the facility and relocate it to Redding, California. The bleacher and bucket seats from the Vacaville location were later incorporated into Redding's Tiger Field during a 2014 renovation that expanded that ballpark's capacity to 1,200 seats.

This profile entry represents a critical data error: Travis Credit Union Park is not a financial services provider of any kind. It was a sports facility, and the website URL provided (a Wikipedia article about a defunct baseball stadium) contains no financial products, services, consumer lending, credit union services, or payday alternative offerings. This company should not be listed on a consumer finance platform.
